caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The 5-digit role mix is a proxy and includes vegetation applicators and residual work outside a pure structural-pest route.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: AI can improve technician productivity without eliminating a licensed field visit; that augmentation is not counted as task replacement.
inputs.pi_dist: Retention varies with route density, local competition, and residential versus commercial mix.
inputs.pi_moat: This is a capture judgment and does not treat physical service durability as an automatic pricing moat.
inputs.t50_years: PCT surveys are industry-reader samples rather than a probability sample of the $1–10M EBITDA band.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: The observed poll percentage is not a nationally representative code-level employer rate and overlaps with other AI uses.
inputs.historical_analogs: Sponsor-backed survey and operational-software evidence may overrepresent technology-engaged operators.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: Not an observed statistic; replace only with a direct target-band owner-demographic source.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Absence of located evidence is not evidence of no succession pressure.
inputs.active_consolidators: The buyer count includes large-company activity and does not establish competitive bid depth for every geography or subsegment.
inputs.buy_mult: S8 is a compiled third-party table, not disclosed bilateral deal terms; normalize owner compensation, recurring revenue, churn, and termite liabilities.
inputs.exit_mult: Requires transferable customer relationships, normalized manager compensation, and sound quality of earnings; it is not a universal platform premium.
inputs.pricing_structure: NAICS includes diverse pests and fumigation; vegetation overlap and local seasonality make national pricing averages weak.
inputs.pi_moat: Licensing supports task and terminal durability; do not treat it as an incremental capture moat without separate evidence that it increases retained savings.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[56]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $139,825 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 561710); class <5: 8,882 firms, avg revenue/firm $236,662 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $37,038 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 2,491 firms, avg revenue/firm $736,951 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $115,333 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 1,373 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,587,316 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $248,415 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 766 firms, avg revenue/firm $4,665,939 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $730,219 -> out of $1-10M band; class 100-499: 70 firms, avg revenue/firm $21,941,586 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $3,433,858 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 21 firms, avg revenue/firm $396,989,381 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $62,128,838 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 70

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: The selected 0.2242 share holds the technician-heavy field core largely nonreplaceable while assigning high automation to office, service, scheduling, bookkeeping, and routine sales work. It is a task estimate using supplied wage weights, not a prediction of headcount reduction.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 561710 ($8,715,666,468) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 561710 ($19,563,962,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

sector.name: Administrative and Support and Waste Management and Remediation Services

source_review.summary: Mechanics pass. The sources support a technician-heavy, durable physical-service thesis with bounded office and routing automation, active consolidation, and the stated multiple table. Disclosed survey, owner-age, labor-mix, buyer-depth, and transaction-comparability limitations remain non-fatal; licensing should not add a separate capture benefit without distinct evidence.

sources.title: 2022 NAICS definitions — Exterminating and Pest Control Services
sources.what_it_supports: Exact 561710 scope: exterminating and controlling birds, mosquitoes, rodents, termites, and other pests, including fumigation, excluding crop and forestry pest control.

sources.title: BLS Occupational Outlook Handbook — Pest Control Workers
sources.what_it_supports: Physical technician duties, customer-site work, pesticide safety, licensing, and 2024–34 employment outlook.

sources.title: PCOs Turn to AI for Task Automation and Marketing Help, Poll Finds
sources.what_it_supports: PCT reader-poll results: 36% not using AI, 24% using it for repetitive-task automation, and 24% for marketing/customer engagement, plus a customer-service scheduling example.

sources.title: Not Just an Advantage, But the Backbone of Pest Management
sources.what_it_supports: PCT/FieldRoutes survey reporting operations-software adoption and automation, scheduling, route-optimization, productivity, and efficiency use cases.
